notes: Twenty-fourth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its materials say the school strives to close the achievement gap through personalized instruction and character education.
Official branding says it is "Striving for academic excellence, cultivating leaders who will rise to the P.E.A.K."
The school emphasizes student growth and community connection as part of its public charter model.
Official pages list Kidada Wynn as executive director.
Official board pages list David Robinson as board chair.
Its board page says the board meets on the second Tuesday of each month.
Official materials describe it as the Charlotte region's first public trade high school.
The school says students choose from 11 trade opportunities while working toward a Future-Ready Core diploma.
Official materials emphasize hands-on learning, strong academics, and real-world career pathways.
The school says it was established by the Aspire Carolinas Foundation.
Official board pages list Melinda Bales as board chair.
Official staff pages list Lafrieeda Busby as executive administrator.
Its mission says the school aims to be the premier school of choice where academic rigor and character excellence cultivate college-ready learners and strengthen community.
Official history pages say the charter was approved by the State Board of Education on February 3, 2000.
The school traces its origins to a community fight to preserve a smaller K-8 structure instead of consolidation.
Its recent messaging says students are shaped into curious thinkers, connected individuals, and courageous leaders.
Official materials say the school expanded into a new 77,000-square-foot facility in 2017 and graduated its first senior class in 2021.
Official administration pages list Scott Lewis as executive director.
Its mission says the school fosters independence, inclusivity, critical thinking, and a sense of global responsibility using Montessori philosophy and Spanish language acquisition.
Official materials say the school was founded in 2003.
The school offers both Spanish Immersion and Spanish Enrichment as two different language-learning pathways.
Official materials say immersion graduates often test out of Spanish 1 and frequently Spanish 2 in high school.
The school describes itself as Wake County's only dual-language Montessori school.
Its board materials say the board meets monthly, typically on the third Thursday at 5:00 p.m.
Its mission says the school cultivates high student achievement in academics, character, and the arts.
Official materials say the school was founded in 1997 in Wilson.
The school highlights a performing arts-based curriculum and a study abroad program that has taken students to nearly all seven continents.
Official materials say the school earned a 2021 National Blue Ribbon School Award.
Its PRIDE Power character program teaches perseverance, respect, integrity, discipline, and excellence.
Official leadership pages list Dr. JoAnne Woodard as founder and executive director and Glenn Reaves as principal.
